skill.name: concept-explainer
skill.description: Uses analogies to explain complex medical concepts in accessible terms.
skill.summary: Uses analogies to explain complex medical concepts in accessible terms.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 56 potential issues (7 high, 34 medium, 15 low). After manual evaluation: all HIGH findings are false positives (weak crypto detection on non-crypto text such as markdown descriptions and argparse configuration; .keys() Python method mistaken for certificate files). The CRITICAL heuristic combination finding (code execution + network + credentials) is also a false positive as no credentials or network calls exist. The medium filesystem finding (scripts/main.py:160 unvalidated output file path) is a real but low-severity concern. External commands in SKILL.md are Python documentation examples in code blocks, not active execution. No credential access, no network calls from code, and no dynamic code execution exist. Overall risk is low.

medium_findings.title: Unvalidated Output File Path
medium_findings.description: The --output parameter writes to a user-specified file path without validating for directory traversal sequences (../). An attacker could potentially write outside the intended workspace directory.

medium_findings.title: External Commands in Documentation
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md contains python commands in markdown code blocks that instruct an AI agent to execute shell commands. While the commands are legitimate (python compilation and script execution), they represent external command execution risk if the agent follows these instructions.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in Documentation (False Positive)
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a GitHub source repository URL. This is attribution, not a network call. No actual HTTP requests are made from the code.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal in Security Checklist (False Positive)
low_findings.description: SKILL.md references '../' in a security checklist describing practices to prevent path traversal, not actual traversal sequences. These are documentation items about what code should NOT do.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged non-crypto text as weak cryptographic algorithms. The flagged lines contain markdown descriptions, argparse configuration, and JSON metadata. No cryptographic operations exist in this skill.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detection (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged documentation text about output discipline, validation commands, and test cases as system reconnaissance. These are legitimate workflow descriptions.

low_findings.title: Certificate/Key File Detection (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged scripts/main.py line 87 for certificate/key files. The actual code is self.ANALOGIES.keys(), a standard Python dictionary method call.

content.user_title: Explain Medical Concepts with Simple Analogies
content.value_statement: Medical terms can be difficult for patients and students to understand. This skill uses everyday analogies like traffic jams and security systems to make complex medical concepts clear and memorable.
actual_capabilities: Explains medical concepts using everyday analogies for different audience levels
Supports three audience levels: patient, child, and student with adjusted language
Lists all available medical concepts on demand
Saves explanations to JSON files for documentation and record keeping
Provides consistent structured output with concept, analogy, and explanation fields
limitations: Only supports five pre-defined medical concepts in the built-in database
Does not fetch or verify medical information from external sources
Child-level simplification uses basic first-sentence truncation only
Output is limited to JSON format without alternative presentation options

use_cases.title: Explain a Diagnosis to a Patient
use_cases.description: A doctor explains thrombosis to a patient using a traffic jam analogy, making a complex medical condition easy to understand without technical jargon.

use_cases.title: Help a Child Understand the Immune System
use_cases.description: A parent or teacher helps a child learn how the immune system works by comparing it to a building security system with guards checking for intruders.

use_cases.title: Reinforce Learning for Medical Students
use_cases.description: A medical student uses antibiotic resistance analogies to reinforce classroom learning with memorable, real-world comparisons.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Concept Explanation for a Patient
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the medical concept of [concept name] to a patient using a simple analogy.
prompt_templates.scenario: A patient needs to understand a medical term during a consultation.

prompt_templates.title: Child-Friendly Explanation
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain [concept name] as if talking to a 10-year-old child. Use a very simple analogy and short sentences.
prompt_templates.scenario: A child needs to understand a family member's health condition or a biology topic.

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Explanation for a Student
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain [concept name] for a medical student. Include the analogy and add technical context about the underlying mechanisms.
prompt_templates.scenario: A medical student needs to understand a concept more deeply with both analogy and scientific detail.

prompt_templates.title: List and Compare Multiple Concepts
prompt_templates.prompt: List all available medical concepts and compare two of them using separate analogies. Explain how they are related.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants to understand how related medical conditions differ from each other.

output_examples.output: The skill returns a structured explanation with the concept name, a traffic jam analogy, and a plain-language explanation comparing blood clots to traffic blocking a highway.

output_examples.input: Explain the immune system to a child
output_examples.output: A simplified explanation comparing the immune system to a building security system. Security guards patrol the building looking for intruders, just like immune cells patrol the body for germs. Sentences are kept short and simple.
A printed list of five medical concepts: thrombosis, immune system, antibiotic resistance, inflammation, and blood pressure. Each concept has a pre-built analogy ready for explanation.

best_practices: Specify the target audience to get the most appropriate explanation level for your situation.
Review generated analogies for medical accuracy before sharing with patients or using in educational materials.
Use the structured JSON output format to save explanations for documentation and record keeping.
anti_patterns: Using the skill for diagnosis or treatment recommendations instead of explanation only.
Assuming analogies are clinically precise without verification from a medical professional.
Requesting explanations for concepts that are not in the pre-defined database without checking the list first.

faq.question: What medical concepts does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports five concepts: thrombosis, immune system, antibiotic resistance, inflammation, and blood pressure.

faq.question: Can I add new medical concepts to the database?
faq.answer: No. The concept database is hardcoded in the Python script and requires code changes to extend. You cannot add concepts through prompts or commands.

faq.question: Is the medical information clinically verified?
faq.answer: The analogies are simplified educational examples. They should not replace professional medical advice or clinical decision-making.

faq.question: What audience levels are available?
faq.answer: Three levels: patient (default), child, and student. Each level adjusts the language complexity of the explanation.

faq.question: How do I save the output to a file?
faq.answer: Use the --output parameter with a file path. For example: python scripts/main.py --concept thrombosis --output result.json

faq.question: Does this skill make network requests?
faq.answer: No. All explanations are generated locally from a built-in database. No external API calls or internet access is required.
